title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the coefficient of static friction, we need to consider the forces acting on the bacon as it starts to slide. The static friction force must be equal to the component of gravitational force parallel to the pan's surface.

title: Identify the Forces
text:
The forces involved are:
$$1$$. Gravitational force acting downwards.
$$2$$. Normal force perpendicular to the pan.
$$3$$. Static friction force opposing the motion.

title: Calculate the Angle
text: The angle \( $$\theta$$ \) at which the bacon starts to slide can be found using the height and length of the pan:\n\( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$\arcsin(\frac{5.0}{23.5})$$ \)

title: Static Friction Formula
text: The coefficient of static friction \( \mu_s \) is given by:\n\( \mu_s $$=$$ \tan(\theta) \)

title: Calculate \( \mu_s \)
text: Using \( \mu_s $$=$$ \tan(\theta) \), calculate the coefficient of static friction.
